topics.topic_en: Formal Definitions of Asymptotic Notations (Big-O, Omega, Theta)
topics.topic_ar: التعريفات الرياضية للرموز التقاربية (Big-O, Omega, Theta)
topics.query_en: Asymptotic notations Big O Big Omega Big Theta formal definition examples
topics.query_ar: شرح Asymptotic Notations Big O Omega Theta خوارزميات
topics.reason: Students struggle with the formal mathematical proofs involving constants (c) and thresholds (n0) to establish upper and lower bounds.

topics.topic_ar: تحليل كفاءة الحالة المتوسطة باستخدام الاحتمالات
topics.query_en: Average case time complexity analysis probability algorithms
topics.query_ar: شرح Average case time complexity تحليل الخوارزميات
topics.reason: Unlike worst-case scenarios, average-case analysis requires applying discrete probability theory and calculating expected values across all possible inputs.

topics.topic_en: Setting up Summations for Dependent Nested Loops
topics.topic_ar: صياغة معادلات الجمع (Summations) للحلقات المتداخلة المعتمدة على بعضها
topics.query_en: Mathematical analysis of non-recursive algorithms summations nested loops dependent variables
topics.query_ar: شرح mathematical analysis of non-recursive algorithms summations
topics.reason: Determining the correct mathematical upper and lower bounds for summations when inner loop limits depend on outer loop variables is highly error-prone.

topics.topic_en: Solving Recurrence Relations using the Master Theorem
topics.topic_ar: حل العلاقات التكرارية باستخدام نظرية الماستر (Master Theorem)
topics.query_en: Master theorem for recurrence relations algorithms examples
topics.reason: Memorizing the specific cases of the Master Theorem and correctly identifying which mathematical case applies to a given recurrence relation can be confusing.

topics.topic_en: Recursion Tree Method for Solving Recurrences
topics.topic_ar: طريقة شجرة التكرار لحل العلاقات التكرارية
topics.query_en: Recursion tree method recurrence relations time complexity
topics.query_ar: شرح Recursion tree method تحليل الخوارزميات
topics.reason: Visualizing recursive calls as a tree and correctly summing the computational costs at each level requires strong abstract thinking and geometric series knowledge.
